rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF STEPHEN J. OUTLAW
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Motion To Compel Petitioner To Respond To Objector'S Request For Production Of Documents (Added For Posting Of The Tentative Ruling)
rulings.Rulings: [2 of 2] Hernandez and Escalante's request for sanctions is GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. The court shall impose a monetary sanction against any party, person, or attorney who unsuccessfully makes or opposes a motion to compel a response to a document demand, unless it finds that the one subject to the sanction acted with substantial justification or that other circumstances make the imposition of the sanction unjust. CCP Section 2031.300(c). Sanctions for failure to provide discovery are available "even though no opposition to the motion was filed, or opposition  was withdrawn, or the requested discovery was provided  after the motion was filed." See Cal. Rules Ct., rule 3.1348(a). Here, the court finds that Outlaw unsuccessfully opposed Hernandez and Escalante's motion, Outlaw failed to act with substantial justification and no other circumstances make the imposition of sanctions unjust. The court is mindful of Outlaw's claim that she was homeless until recently. But Outlaw is the petitioner in this proceeding and, as such, she must act with diligence and in good faith. Hernandez and Escalante gave her additional time to comply and, as noted, she failed to act in good faith. The court orders Outlaw to pay sanctions to Hernandez and Escalante in the amount of $1,050.00. To the extent Hernandez and Escalante seek sanctions in excess of $1,050.00, their request is denied. Hernandez and Escalante shall lodge with the clerk in Department 204 by the time set for this hearing a proposed order consistent with this tentative. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on the day before the hearing. Outlaw is ordered to lodge with the clerk in Department 204 a proposed order consistent with this tentative by the time set for this hearing. Remote appearance is available in conformity with the current General Order of the Probate and Mental Health Division, available on the court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/divisions/probate. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925 and passcode 722809. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925 and passcode 722809. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF STEPHEN J. OUTLAW
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Petitioner To Respond To Objector'S Request For Production Of Documents
rulings.Rulings: [1 of 2] This tentative ruling is issued by Hon. Joseph M. Quinn. Petitioner Cruz Outlaw is the personal representative of the estate of her deceased husband, Stephen J. Outlaw. Jose A. Hernandez is Cruz Outlaw's son and Enna S. Escalante is Hernandez's wife. Cruz Outlaw filed a Petition for Return of Property to the Estate Pursuant to California Probate Code Section 850 and for an Order Finding Financial and Physical Elder Abuse Pursuant to Welfare and institution's Code Section 15610.70 et. Seq [sic]. Hernandez and Escalante are Respondents on that petition. The court denied the elder abuse claims without prejudice; the remainder of the petition is pending. In the remaining claim, Outlaw alleges that Hernandez and Escalante took over a coin collection that belonged to the estate, sold it, and used the proceeds to buy their home; they must now reimburse the estate or surrender equity in their home to the estate. Hernandez and Escalante deny the allegations. Trial is set for May 10, 2022. Now before the court is Hernandez and Escalante's Motion to Compel Petitioner to Respond to Objector's Request for Production of Documents, Set One The motion to compel a response to the document demand is GRANTED. Hernandez and Escalante properly served their demand on Outlaw and the time for Outlaw to respond expired before Hernandez and Escalante filed their motion. Outlaw failed to timely respond to the document demand. Her September 14, 2021 response was untimely. Moreover, Outlaw's September 14 response is a sham, likely to avoid the motion to compel which had been filed almost three weeks earlier. Outlaw's statements of inability to comply do not conform with CCP Section 2031.230. For example, Outlaw makes no showing of any effort-let alone a good faith effort-to comply with the document demand (perhaps by reaching out to Hernandez and Escalante to gain access to her personal items stored with them). Outlaw's objection that the document demand was unverified is without foundation; document demands need not be verified. Her assertion that Hernandez and Escalante's motion fails because it is not written is belied by the record, which includes Hernandez and Escalante's written motion. Outlaw shall provide a Code-compliant response to Hernandez and Escalante's document demand within ten days of service of this order.
